rows.notes: Clear page title centered at top. Legible handwriting and no monetary figures on this row. Vertical ruling lines not needed for this header.

Amount '8-0-0' is clearly aligned in the pounds/shillings/pence columns. The name in the description is somewhat ambiguous (handwritten surname looks like 'Trewsey' or similar); chosen reading based on letter shapes but uncertainty in one or two letters.

rows.description: Recd Balance of Mr Jackson Rent pr Mch: 1765 to Mch: 1768
rows.notes: Description largely legible; dates '1765 to Mch:1768' clear. Amount '103-14-11' is clearly written and aligns well with vertical column rulings (shillings and pence within valid ranges).
Description reads as 'Mr Grindin' (final letters a little compressed but context suggests a surname). Amount '8-0-0' is clear and columns unambiguous.

rows.description: Second Paymt of Dr Thorbridge for ye year 1766.
rows.notes: Description interpreted as 'Second Paymt of Dr Thorbridge for ye year 1766' — the surname is slightly uncertain (could be Thorndige/Thorbridge) but the phrase and year are legible. Amount '100-0-0' is clearly written and aligned.

Surname and first word are uncertain in the script (reads like 'Benson' or 'Burron'); chosen 'Benson Midwinter present 1767' as best-fit. Amount '60-12-4' is readable and columns align, shillings/pence are within valid ranges.

A number-only row immediately under a short ruling line; appears to be a subtotal or carried figure. The digits '340-7-3' are clear and align with the pounds/shillings/pence columns. No description present on the ledger line itself.
Number-only subtotal '126-10-0' clearly written and well aligned. No descriptive text on the left.
Number-only subtotal '154-10-0' clearly visible and columns unambiguous.
Number-only subtotal '105-2-4' clearly written. Pence and shilling values are within valid range.

Heavier underlined total appears beneath the group of subtotals. The reading '726-9-11' is visible though slightly faded; alignment with column rulings used to confirm digit boundaries. There is some faintness in the pence digit which reduces confidence slightly.

rows.description: In Manner ex Deb: an: Precedentij
rows.notes: Description is a Latinized phrase (handwritten as 'In Manner ex Deb: an: Precedentij'); readable but slightly stylized. Amount reads as '195-10-4 1/2' — the halfpenny symbol (loop after pence) is visible and interpreted as 0.5. Column alignment supports the reading. Minor uncertainty in the final fractional mark's clarity led to lowered confidence.

Final summed line reads 'Summa Receptorum' (heading for total receipts) with amount that arithmetically corresponds to adding the heavy total (726-9-11) and the prior line (195-10-4 1/2) => 922-0-3 1/2. The pence fraction is interpreted as a halfpenny (0.5) from the small loop after the pence figure. The final fraction/last pence digit in the scan is slightly faint, so confidence is moderate but consistent with column arithmetic.
